combos.notes: Basic resourceless hitconfirm.623MP has the most combo range against both standing and crouching.
Only works at point-blank against a standing opponent.

2-hit confirms are more flexible. 623MP is still the most consistent.623LP gives a closer knockdown but whiffs more easily.623HP deals more damage, but is particularly inconsistent against crouching opponents.
More damage than regular 623P.
For when too far away to connect with 623MP.236KK is a safejump setup against slow (7f) anti-airs. Bait fast ones with empty jump.

combos.combo: (L >) L > LP > DRC~LP, (Drive Rush Combo) 2HP > (DRC~4HP, MK) > (Special) 2HP > DRC~4HP, 2MP > SA1 2HP > 214PP > W.SA2 2HP > (DRC~4HP) > 214MP > SA3
combos.notes: Stay close with 2LP or a shorter confirm to link into 2HP.If too far then link into MK instead.
Easier input: 2LP > 236LP~236P
Without Windclad, SA2 whiffs after long confirms.
Deals more damage off a shorter confirm.

Start point-blank against a standing opponent so 623PP hits only once.When not point-blank it's possible to do this combo using more 2LPs or a 2-hit confirm, but it's much more difficult to set up the spacing.
Possible to confirm by looking for the counter-hit message.SA2 whiffs at long range.
Possible to confirm by looking for the counter-hit message.
Interruptible on block, but leads to better rewards.Needs a knockdown advantage of 5LK +5 / 5LP +5~6 / 2LP +6~7.Refer to the frame advantage table on the Strategy page for ways to accomplish this.
W.236MK, 214LP is +2 on wakeup and loopable in the corner.W.236LK is more consistent at range but loses 2 frames of advantage, leading to different followup options.
Slightly more damage, but leads to a far knockdown.
Point blank combo. Misses a hit when done after blocked W.236K, resulting in less damage.

combos.combo: (L >) L > LP > DRC~LP, (Drive Rush Combo) 2HP > (DRC~4HP, MK) > (Special) 2HP > DRC~4HP, 2MP > SA1/W.SA2 2HP > (DRC~4HP) > 214MP > SA3 (Corner) 2HP > (DRC~4HP, MK) > W.236HK > W.623HP/SA1/*.SA2 (Corner) 2HP > W.236HK > W.623PP > j.PPP (Corner) 2HP > W.236HK > W.623PP > W.j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: Stay close with 2LP or a shorter confirm to link into 2HP.If too far then link into MK instead.
Windclad gives SA2 enough range to be confirmed from 3 hits.
Late hit Tomahawk Buster followup.The opponent must get hit standing at the correct range.After a blocked W.236KK start with 2LP.
Opponent must reach the corner while in the air.

combos.combo: L > L > LP > W.236KK > W.623PP > *.j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: With 3 Windclad it's possible to avoid burning out with a microwalk before W.623PP.
Corner variant of the late-hit Tomahawk Buster combo.SA1 only works if the opponent gets hit standing.
Spending some drive gauge.
Drive dump into SA1 or SA1.

combos.combo: L > 2LP > 2LP > W.623MP(1), W.236KK, W.623PP > j.PPP, SA2
combos.notes: Full resource dump.
Slightly more damaging against a standing opponent. SA1 needs additional stocks.
Possible to confirm by looking for the counter-hit message.Windclad SA2 always reaches unlike the windless version.
Unsafe and not confirmable.Useful as a combo ender when 2HP is too slow to hit in time, such as after a counter-hit Light attack.
Lighter versions give a closer knockdown.2HP/4HP > 236LK is safe against Drive Impact between hits on block. It can also be spaced to have an advantage of +1 from 2HP, or ±0 from 4HP.
214MP is +2 on hit and doesn't knock the opponent down, which means they can't Drive Reversal the mixup.
214HP doesn't combo after 2HP or 5HP without a counter-hit or Drive Rush.
Lily's highest damage meterless special move.Mostly useful near the corner or to close out rounds.
Gapless blockstring from 2HP, 4HP or 6HP.SA2 doesn't reach when done from the tip of 2HP/4HP range.
Use at extremely long ranges where other special moves may not reach to combo.
Good for closing out rounds with 2-3 Drive bars available.If more than 3 Drive bars are available then use a Drive Rush combo instead.
Combo into 1 bar of drive damage.When blocked by burned out JP or Marisa, 6HP > DI can't be interrupted by 3f armor.
Confirming this off 2HP is particularly useful during burnout.2MP is good for interrupting Drive Rush, but it's possible for the combo to drop after hitting an extended hurtbox.
Requires near point blank range.

Less range than SA1 but more range than SA2.If a combo doesn't use Drive Impact or a Drive Rush Cancel, canceling directly into SA3 deals more damage than using 214MP but loses out on gaining a Windclad stock.
Typically combos directly into Special, SA, or Drive Rush.If close enough it's possible to do another 2LP or 5LP first.
+3 on block.
+2 on block.
+2 on block. There is a gap but it is safe against Drive Impact.
Builds and uses Windclad.
Builds and uses Windclad.
+1 on block.Lighter versions combo from further away and give a closer knockdown.
Fewer followup options than Condor Spire, so mainly only used to close out rounds.
Deals the same damage as 214PP but doesn't drop the combo at max range.
Good for closing out rounds with 2-3 Drive bars available.With only 1 Windclad and more than 3 Drive bars, use a Drive Rush combo instead.
Liable to drop when used as a whiff punish against extended hurtboxes.
Huge corner carry means this can be started from almost the middle of the stage.SA1 works if the opponent even slightly grazes the corner while in the air.
May not combo from the tip of 2HP or 4HP.Replace both specials with Medium or Light versions to increase the combo range.
Requires 3 Windclad to build enough juggle height.
W.236KK deals no extra damage, but launches higher than W.236HK.
Starting with Windclad adds damage to the dive, but it still doesn't launch high enough to link into SA.

Single hit confirmable, anti Drive Impact combo.Buffer the SA3 input and press the button on reaction to the opponent's life bar going down (unless they are in burnout).Also effective against opponents that try to punish 214LP with Drive Rush. Hold 214MP for a moment to avoid the perfect parry, then release it to hit them out of Drive Rush.
Huge range whiff punish or anti-air or in general just anti-not-blocking.Particularly useful for keeping Jamie sober.Also hits opponents trying to bait anti-airs with an early whiffed divekick.
Condor Spire must hit towards the end of its active frames:623LP: 10~13f, 623MP: 12~13f
Condor Spire must hit with its last 4 active frames (10~13f).
Condor Spire must hit with its last 4 active frames (10~13f).Works slightly further away from the corner than Tomahawk Buster.
Condor Spire must hit towards the end of its active frames:623LP: 5~13f, 623MP: 7~13f, 623HP: 9~13f
Condor Spire must hit towards the end of its active frames:623PP: 5~13f, SA2: 11~13f, SA1 13f
Punishes projectiles or an opponent that's just not blocking.
Projectile punish near the corner.Works as long as the opponent reaches the corner while in the air.
Projectile punish with 2 Windclad.
Projectile punish with 2+ Windclad.SA1 requires 3 Windclad.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.Can also start with the much less practical W.236HK.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.The drive gauge generated by W.236MK can prevent burnout.
Condor Spire must hit less than 3 times.Requires a maxed out drive gauge and a micro-walk between Condor Spires to build extra drive gauge.
Condor Spire must hit 1 time.
Condor Spire must hit 1 time.Unlike the 2-hit variant this links into SA1.
Condor Spire must hit 1 time.Restocks the spent Windclad.
Condor Spire must hit 1 time.
Condor Spire must be spaced to hit only once.Technically not a crumple but leads to the same combo routes.
Leads to the same combos as a PC 5HP > DRC~2HP crumple, but dealing ~800-900 less damage. The opponent is launched higher so adjustments may need to be made.
As above, but makes the counter-hit confirmable.On non-counter-hit, stop after the DRC to pressure their landing, with a potential left-right mixup if the spacing is right.

Hold punch and release it on reaction to the counter-hit to juggle the opponent.On non-counter-hit, delay the release until the opponent lands to make them block it, ideally with its later active frames to make it safer on block.Punishable if the opponent takes the hit with an empty jump and lands with an invincible reversal. Be extra careful against an opponent with SA3 ready, and maybe consider baiting them with 214LP.
Hold PP and release it on reaction to the counter-hit.Particularly useful for confirming directly into SA2 for huge damage.On non-counter-hit keep PP held to be completely safe.
On non-counter-hit 236HK is usually advantageous on block, but vulnerable to anti-airs.236MK is better on block after hitting the opponent closer to the ground.
Kills characters with regular health if their health bar is yellow.If the opponent has just enough health to survive, try to sneak in a Condor Wind at some point before they jump.
Must catch the opponent high up to link the SA.
Works from a jump in any direction, but must connect close to the opponent.Useful as a backward jump air-to-air against an opponent trying to cross-up.
Particularly useful when clipping a forward jumping opponent during a neutral jump.
Consistent from a forward jump. Requires point-blank distance from a neutral jump.
SA1 works slightly further away from the corner than SA2.Condor Dive can be delayed to avoid whiffing under the opponent. The delay also closes some distance to the corner from a forward jump.
Use 623LP for more consistency and a closer knockdown.
Must catch the opponent high up to link the SA.
Punishable by empty jump into anti-air.Condor Spire must hit all 3 times.Delaying the cancel makes this more consistent off a high hit.
Must hit the opponent high up so Condor Spire only hits 2 times.With more Windclad stocks, W.236HK is better.
Punishable by empty jump into anti-air.Leads into big damage with more resources near the corner. Refer to the Crumple Starter section.
Place more priority on building Windclad if the extra damage is enough to make this kill.
Having Windclad for the dive lets the SA link from a lower height anti-air.
Works from a jump in any direction.Useful as a backward jump air-to-air against an opponent trying to cross-up.
Particularly useful when clipping a forward jumping opponent during a neutral jump.
Must be fairly close to the opponent to reach from a neutral jump.
Condor Dive can be delayed to avoid whiffing under the opponent. The delay also closes some distance to the corner from a forward jump.
More consistent and damaging than the windless version.Use 623LP for more consistency and a closer knockdown.
More consistent and damaging than the windless version.
Having Windclad for the dive lets the SA link from a lower height anti-air.
Lots of corner carry, but requires quite a high hit to land in time for the pick-up.
Must catch the opponent high up. Use 623LP for consistency after 236LK.
W.236KK adds 160 damage and guarantees enough height for SA2, or SA1 with 3 Windclad.
Must connect with the opponent near the peak of their jump.More height allows for a heavier Tomahawk Buster.
Must connect with the opponent near the peak of their jump.
Must connect with the opponent at the peak of their jump.Use 623LP for consistency after 236LK.

combos.combo: AA W.623LP/MP/HP, W.236LK, W.623PP > j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: Must connect with the opponent at the peak of their jump.

combos.combo: AA W.623LP/MP/HP, W.236KK, W.623PP > j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: Burning some more drive to make it slightly easier.
Must be close to the opponent so the first hit of 2HP connects.Works with either 5LP for more damage, or 2LP for more range.2HP can also be linked from a Meaty or Counter-hit DR~5LK.

The extra hitstun from Drive Rush makes 214HP combo from any of 2HP, 4HP, 5HP or 6HP..DR~2HP/4HP/6HP > 214MP is a gapless blockstring.DR~2HP > 214HP is also gapless but leaves the opponent closer, making it more punishable.
Combos from any of 2HP, 4HP, 5HP or 6HP.
Combo into 1 bar of drive damage.2HP makes it a gapless blockstring that still deals 1 bar of drive damage.
Typically used to go into 623HP or another Drive Rush.
Slightly more damage than 5MK into SA.
Must be close unless Windclad, which makes it work from maximum range.
More damage than 214MP > SA3 after a raw Parry Drive Rush, but less damage after a Drive Rush Cancel.

A slight damage boost for Tomahawk Buster enders.Also a way to confirm non-canceled DR~2HP at close range. Shorten the light chain by 1 attack to account for distance.5LK > 5LP > DR~2LP, 2HP always works.5LK > 5LP > 623MP works at close range.5LK > 5LP > W.623P sets up late-hit Tomahawk Buster combos.

A 2 to 5 frame micro-walk after 4HP builds a pixel of drive gauge to Drive Rush Cancel twice without burning out, optionally leading into a 3rd. Micro-walk 5LP can be used instead of 5LK for a little more damage, but this reduces the timing window from 4 frames to 3 frames, making it less consistent.Chaining into 5LP builds a tiny bit more meter at the cost of a tiny amount of damage.
A frame perfect variant of the micro-walk combo that does even more damage.5LP/2LP can be skipped to get slightly more damage out of triple drive rush combos.
2LP has more range, but 5LP deals slightly more damage.Can also use 5LK when close for easier timing.

A counter-hit allows for longer range confirms.2HP has more reach but deals less damage due to only hitting once.From maximum range it's possible to follow up with any of 236KK, 214PP, W.236LK, or a Drive Rush Cancel.
Works if 4HP hits any later than its first active frame.Can be set up with a knockdown advantage of +25~27.The easiest setup is SA1 against a grounded opponent in the corner (+27).

Works if 3HP hits any later than its first active frame.Can be set up with a knockdown advantage of +36~37. Typically works after windless 236MK/HK (+37) or 623HP/MP/LP (+36) hitting on or close to the ground.
Works if 3HP hits on its last active frame.Can be set up with a knockdown advantage of +37. Typically works after windless 236MK/HK (+37) hitting on or close to the ground.
Meterless and driveless whiff punish that almost always reaches.
Close range punish.360LP is less efficient than a regular combo.
Standard punish on whiff or block into a special move or SA.
Builds more meter and drive gauge than a special cancel.
The double Drive Rush version works without a delay, but Condor Spire will only hit twice for less damage.Unlike the previous combo this can be used as a burnout combo with less than 3 Drive bars.

Corner carry from a high launching starter. Mostly useful in situations where 6HP doesn't reach.2HP must hit with its first active frame.When using the PC DI, 8j.HK, 6HP starter, a delayed 6HP is required against anyone except Blanka, Dee Jay, E.Honda, JP, Marisa, Sagat or Zangief.

Full drive dump combo.For SA1 do the 2HP version and link it after 4HP instead of canceling.Make sure to use a 4HP starter when it reaches instead of 6HP not just for extra damage, but to avoid accidentally crossing under the opponent.
An extremely efficient use of meter.The drive rush extension deals less damage but builds more meter.
+44 safe jump setup from a high launching starter.2HP must hit with its first active frame.Still a safe jump when performed from the PC DI, 8j.HK, 6HP starter.
Generates and immediately uses Windclad.
An efficient 4 drive route into SA.
Drive dump extension of the Condor Wind route.Burnout can be avoided with a 2-3 frame micro-walk before W.623PP, input as 623[6]~dl.PP.
Using 4HP allows the previous combo to link into SA2.
+44 safe jump setup.Also possible with exactly 1 Windclad stock by delaying the cancel after W.236KK.
Only works right in the corner.Refer to 1 stock combos in the Windclad tab for followups.Basic enders are 214HP for even more wind, or W.236HK > SA1/SA2 to cash out.
A somewhat expensive +44 safe jump setup.
Use MK when not already in the corner to carry them there.SA2 also works, but 214PP > SA2 routes are better.
An alternate drive dump route that builds an extra stock for the dive.Requires almost full drive gauge so hitting with 5HP builds it back to over 4 bars.
Using 5MK makes the previous combo work with SA1

Requires extremely specific positioning.Lily's front toe during the hitstop of 5HP must be 1-2 marks out from the first thick grid line on the training stage, or 0-2 marks for SA2.Works after blocking an OD reversal from Ken, Luke, Juri, Dee Jay, or anything else that lands that far out.The drive gauge built during the combo can prevent burnout.
Meterless and driveless whiff punish that reaches even further than the windless version.
Meterless drive dump.A delay is needed for W.623HP to fully connect after double Drive Rush.

Extremely efficient way to combo into SA1.The second drive rush extension still works without a delay, but Condor Spire will only hit twice for less damage.Make sure to use a 4HP starter when it reaches instead of 6HP not just for extra damage, but to avoid accidentally crossing under the opponent.
Midscreen wind and drive dump.
Full burnout variant picking up with the longer ranged 6HP.
Corner only SA1 variant of the above.Carries over half stage distance.
Even more corner carry.
An efficient 4 drive route into SA.Difficult to generate height with only 1 Windclad - usually requires 4HP instead of and/or a delayed cancel.
Drive dump extension of the Condor Wind route.Burnout can be avoided with a 2-3 frame micro-walk before W.623PP, input as 623[6]~dl.PP.
Drive dump route into SA2.
W.623HP is a +44 safe jump setup.
Drive dump version of the above.

combos.combo: PC 5HP, dl.4HP > W.236KK, W.623HP, W.623LP/(W.623PP > j.PPP
combos.notes: Requires a specific distance from the corner. On the training stage, Lily's front toe must be between the 1st and 3rd marks after the large grid line.4HP must not be done too close to the opponent. Delaying it allows the opponent to fall backwards to the correct range.
Requires more drive gauge than the above but works from further away.
Requires more drive gauge than the above.
If the opponent is right in the corner, walk back to hit with the tip of 4HP and combo using 623LP623MP requires some space behind the opponent.
More stocks means more leniency, which means heavier Tomahawk Busters.

Requires extremely specific positioning.Lily's front toe during the hitstop of 5HP must be within 1 mark of first thick grid line on the training stage.SA1 requires more precise positioning and a delayed cancel.623MP is more lenient, especially for SA1, but loses 160 damage.
Optimal jab punish when too far to link 2MK.Otherwise link 5MK/2MK > DRC~2HP into one of the other combos below.

combos.combo: PC LP/5LK > DRC~LP, 6~5MK > DRC~2HP > DRC~4HP > 214MP > SA3
combos.notes: Micro-walk variant of the above. Walk for 2-3 frames (2f timing window) to build meter for a third drive rush.
Basic meterless follow-up.
Must start with full drive gauge to build enough to finish the combo.
Basic 1 bar combo.
1 bar drive dump.
Basic 2 bar combo.
An extremely efficient use of meter.
A rare case of 4HP not being the optimal starter. Must start with full drive gauge and then micro-walk to build a pixel back.The optional 5LP builds meter but lowers damage.
Frame perfect variant of the previous combo.
214MP results in less damage but builds more meter and a Windclad stock.
THE punish combo. Builds about 60% of a Super bar.

combos.combo: PC j.HK, 2HP > DRC~4HP, 6~5LK > (5LP) > DRC~LP, 2HP > DRC~(LP, )2HP > 214MP > SA3
combos.notes: Optimal meter building micro-walk combo. Builds a little over 70% of a Super bar.

combos.combo: PC j.HK, 2HP > DRC~4HP, 6~5MK > DRC~(LP, )2HP > DRC~(LP, )2HP > 214MP > SA3
combos.notes: The most optimal, situational, and difficult combo that you're never going to land in a match.
Basic meterless follow-up.
Must start with full drive gauge to build enough to finish the combo.
A Crumple combo that outdamages other midscreen 0-3 Drive SA1 combos.
Windclad corner carry into SA1.
Drive dump variant of the above.

combos.combo: (PC DR~4HP, 2HP) >> (DRC~4HP, MK) > W.236HK, W.623PP > W.j.PPP, SA1
combos.notes: Outdamages all but the most difficult Crumple combo.The Drive Rush extension results in less damage.
Basic 2 bar combo.
Outdamages the above when 2 Windclad stocks are available.
Outdamages the above when 2 Windclad stocks are available.

combos.combo: PC j.HK, 2HP > DRC~4HP, 6~5LK > (5LP) > DRC~LP, 2HP > DRC~4HP, 2MP > W.SA2
combos.notes: Must start with full drive gauge and then micro-walk to build a pixel back.The optional 5LP builds meter but lowers damage.

combos.combo: PC j.HK, 2HP > DRC~4HP, 6~5MK > DRC~(LP, )2HP > DRC~4HP, 2MP > W.SA2
combos.notes: Frame perfect variant of the previous combo.
Windclad corner carry into SA2.
Drive dump variant of the above.
Burns more drive gauge to make the previous combo work with only 2 Windclad stocks.

Situational punish from any cancelable move on an opponent near the corner.This route and its variants below are optimal with minimum Windclad or for meter building. Extra stocks make more damaging W.236HK/KK routes available.
More optimal against standing opponents.
Squeezes out a little more damage from a heavy starter.

combos.combo: PC HP > DRC~6HP, 5LP > W.623HP, W.623PP > *.j.PPP, SA1
combos.notes: 6HP forces stand, making this optimization work on crouching opponents.

combos.combo: PC DI, 4HP/2HP > (Heavy Starter Combo) > SA3/SA1 DRC~4HP/2HP > DRC~4HP, 2MP > SA1 214MP > SA3 DRC~LP, 2HP > 214MP > SA3 DRC~4HP, MK > DRC~(LP, )2HP > 214MP > SA3
combos.notes: Keeps the opponent standing for a combo into SA3 or a reset.Also optimal for a midscreen full drive dump into SA1.

Refer to the Crumple Starter Combo section.Damage will be roughly 90-100% of the listed values.Launches very high so can be used with any crumple combo, even those that normally need to launch with 4HP or 2HP.j.2HP launches slightly higher but j.HK has more horizontal reach.
Consistent on Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat and Zangief.Other characters need a delayed 6HP (2f window) unless they reach the corner before 236KK hits.
Corner carry followup.Consistent on Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat and Zangief.Skipping 236KK is consistent on everyone for 40 less damage.When close to the corner 214PP is better than 236KK.
Deals 2.5 bars of drive damage. Very useful for burning out the opponent. The opponent recovers some drive during 4HP so in some cases it may be better to do DI straight into DI to guarantee burnout.
Delay has a 3f timing window.9j.2HP switches sides in the corner or from long range.
Delay has a 2f timing window.9j.2HP switches sides in the corner or from long range.
A simple way to go into SA2 without using any extra drive gauge.
+44 safe jump setup.
Leads into the same j.MP combos as above.Deals about 400 less damage due to skipping j.2HP
Refer to the Crumple Starter Combo section.Launches very high so can be used with any crumple combo, even those that normally need to launch with 4HP or 2HP.
j.MP just before landing. Easier to time based on how high DI hits.Does not work at all at minimum height.Leads to the same combos as Crumple > 6HP.
Must hit the opponent at the maximum height of 6HP.The timing window is about 3-4 frames, but varies depending on how high the DI hit.
Must hit the opponent at the maximum height of 6HP.The timing window is about 3-4 frames, but varies depending on how high the DI hit.
Refer to the Crumple Starter Combo section.Damage will be roughly 70-75% of the listed values.
Leads into the same juggle combos as PC DI > 8j.HK if the opponent has a long enough travel time to the corner.
Simple drive and meter dump from any stun.
A slightly more damaging route. Manually judge the walk distance so Tomahawk Buster connects late as possible. SA2 requires very precise positioning.
SA1 variant of the above. Also makes SA2 easier at the cost of damage.

combos.combo: Blocked DI (Stun),214LP, 214[PP], 44, 2HP > W.623HP, W.623PP > W.j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: A more complicated full drive dump into SA.

combos.combo: Unblocked DI (Stun),214[LP](2), 44, 2HP > W.623HP, W.623PP > W.j.PPP, SA1/SA2
combos.notes: The same as above, but using less drive gauge due to the extra time provided by the hit.
Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat and Zangief only.DI must be blocked right in the corner to set up the back jump spacing.
Anyone EXCEPT Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat or Zangief.DI must hit a grounded opponent right in the corner.Against the 4 named characters this requires a frame perfect j.HK.
SA3 is not compatible with Windclad juggles, so this route is still necessary.
Delay has a 3f timing window.9j.2HP switches sides in the corner or from long range.W.623MP is only consistent in the corner or after a crossup.
Delay has a 3f timing window.9j.2HP switches sides in the corner or from long range.Delaying j.MP is not necessary.
A simple way to go into SA2 without using any extra drive gauge.
Requires a frame perfect delay on the second j.MP. It's possible to visually confirm the height before going into SA2.
+44 safe jump setup.Against Dee Jay, JP, or Marisa use j.2HP instead.
+44 safe jump setup. Works better than 623HP midscreen due to less knockback.
Situationally optimal with less than 3 drive bars and no SA.DR~2HP should be delayed to hit twice. W.236MK must only hit twice.

combos.combo: PC DI, 8j.HK, dl.6HP, 214PP, W.623PP > W.j.PPP > SA1/SA2
combos.notes: Drive dump route into SA1 or SA2.
Must hit the opponent at the maximum height of 6HP.The timing window is about 3-4 frames, but varies depending on how high the DI hit.
Simple drive and meter dump from any stun.j.HK requires starting with at least 2 Windclad, or 1 Windclad if the opponent has a long travel time to the wall.
More optimal. Manually judge the walk distance so Tomahawk Buster connects as late as possible.
Drive
Standard starter.Any combo listed with this starter can be directly swapped to use any other starter listed below.
Slightly more corner carry. Less damage for combos that use resources.When close to the opponent, walk forwards instead of dash to sacrifice some corner carry to build drive gauge.
Building Windclad mid-combo without spending drive gauge.The opponent must be very close to the corner, so this can't punish forward moving reversals like Ken's OD Dragon Punch.

Close-range or corner starter that deals 100 more damage but builds less drive gauge.Can be used in place of 6HP in any combo if close enough or near the corner.4HP launches slightly higher than 6HP, making it required for some combos.

Long-range whiff punish starter using a non-canceled Drive Rush.Loses some damage due to DR scaling.2HP launches even higher than 4HP and is required by some combos.For maximum launch height 2HP must connect with its first active frame, hence the delay.

Full-screen whiff punish starter done by canceling into Drive Rush instead (e.g. Dhalsim limbs).Doing this close enough keeps the opponent grounded for a Heavy Starter Combo, which is required for SA3.

Point blank combo that builds Windclad, sometimes leading to a more optimal combo.Can also be used to build 2 Windclad in one combo with a 214HP/PP followup.5MK and 2MK are more consistent midscreen due to the special cancel moving towards the opponent.

Standard Drive Impact starter. A delay is sometimes needed to avoid launching the opponent too high.Other air normals can also be used to adjust the launch height. j.2HP provides more juggle time due to hitting 1 frame later, but it has less horizontal range so doesn't always reach. j.MK sacrifices damage to consistently set up a launch slightly higher than 4HP. Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat and Zangief are taller so get hit 1 frame early, resulting in a lower launch height from 6HP. This is good in some cases as it sets up the correct height for certain combos without requiring a delay. In other cases make sure to use j.2HP, which provides the exact same height as j.HK does against other characters.
Whether to dash depends on the distance the opponent travels to the wall.
Works when the opponent travels a long distance to the wall.
Technically not a crumple, but leads into the same combos.
Also technically not a crumple, but leads into the same combos.
Startup
Less damage than DR~4HP but conserves some drive gauge.
Less damage again, but can burn the opponent out more easily, especially when combined with a SA combo ender.The typical follow-up route is j.HK, 6HP into combo ender.

Keeps the opponent grounded for SA3 and gives them less time to recover drive gauge during the combo.Does work with 2HP but the longer animation lets the opponent recover more drive.4HP > SA2/SA3 does not allow the opponent to recover any drive gauge at all.4HP > SA1 lets them recover 1 pixel of drive, but this can be avoided with 5HP > SA1.

For burning out the opponent without using SA.The opponent regains a tiny amount of drive during 4HP. If it looks like they might recover enough to avoid burnout then skip 4HP and go directly into a second DI.
Optimal damage for SA3 combos.Can punish 2 frames faster if the opponent's attack is parried.
Situational starter if the opponent whiffs something during the charge.After a blocked max range 2HP this can dodge and punish some Drive Reversals.
A faster option that deals a bit less damage than 4HP.
Optimal damage for corner combos into SA1 or SA2.Otherwise useful for conserving drive gauge.
Sometimes the simplest option is the best one.Faster than 5HP at close range, slower at long range.
SA2 requires Windclad to combo from a reasonable range.
Alternatively cancel MK directly into a special move.
Works from a longer range than the previous starter.
Point blank punish.
Huge damage using little resources.
